text:
is a fictional character from the Rebuild of Evangelion film series. He is the leader of the SEELE organization.

Profile

The origins and age of Keel are unknown. In the past, he used his power and influence at the UN to create NERV to carry out the Human Instrumentality Project. Keel does not physically appear in the Rebuild of Evangelion, appearing as a monolith.

History

Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone

He is represented as a monolith in Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone. Keel, as part of the SEELE council, meets with Gendo Ikari the day after the destruction of the Fourth Angel. After destroying the Sixth Angel, he speaks with Kaworu Nagisa at Tabgha Base about how everything is happening according to the Dead Sea Scrolls. 

Evangelion: 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance

It is not until Evangelion: 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance that he is credited by name. He calls Evangelion Mark.06 the true Evangelion and expresses the council's intentions to use it to resurrect Lilith to further SEELE's plans.

Evangelion 3.0: You Can (Not) Redo

Keel's monolith appears in Evangelion 3.0: You Can (Not) Redo, being silent during his first appearance. As the monoliths are turned off one by one by Kōzō Fuyutsuki, while Gendo thanks SEELE for their guidance of humanity. Keel accepts his death with dignity, saying that their wish will be fulfilled. He prays for the peaceful unification of all souls before Fuyutsuki turns him off.
